title: Complexity Classes, Reductions, and Feasibility Boundaries · Invariant Recall
summary: Explain why a requested exact optimizer needs changed scope or a bounded alternative. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a feasibility and reduction decision memo after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate optimization questions to decision form without losing the threshold meaning.
Describe polynomial verification and solution concepts without claiming unresolved equalities.
Map instances and answers through a polynomial reduction in the correct hardness direction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Decision Problems correctness-and-cost proof for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Decision Problems assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

stimulus.title: Feasibility Communication case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Compare input sizes 40, 80, and 160; define the counted operation, predict the growth ratio, then challenge the claim with a reversed or duplicate-heavy input.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain computational limits with options and evidence rather than jargon or certainty. Artifact under review: a feasibility and reduction decision memo.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Feasibility Communication: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Compare input sizes 50, 100, and 200; define the counted operation, predict the growth ratio, then challenge the claim with a reversed or duplicate-heavy input.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Explain computational limits with options and evidence rather than jargon or certainty.
